product.name: Claude Operator (claude-decide)
product.description: A Claude Code skill/plugin that acts as an autonomous product manager and developer — continuously researches a target codebase, generates PRDs, collaborates with the user, and executes improvements via an on-disk state machine cycle.
product.problem: Developers leave significant value unrealized due to idle time — Claude Operator eliminates this by continuously iterating on code without relying on the user for direction.

features: Onboarding with automated repo analysis and user interview
7 parallel research agents (code auditor, product gap, security, market, customer value, experimentation, analytics)
PRD generation with fixed template
5-lens PRD self-critique
Interactive collaboration phase for PRD approval
Autonomous execution via superpowers brainstorm-plan-execute pipeline
Stuck detection and recovery after 10+ failed attempts
On-disk state machine with persistent memory, backlog, and cycle logs
Force mode for fully autonomous operation
Meta-research fallback after 3 stagnant cycles
Graceful stop via stop file or Ctrl+C
Bash launcher script for force mode
/decide slash command for invoking operator from Claude Code
Secure force mode launcher with shell injection fix, temp file prompt construction, paused state handling, and max-cycles safety limit
Quick onboarding mode — repo analysis pre-fills all 11 fields with confidence scores, user confirms via summary table instead of 11 sequential questions
Progress indicators — phase banners, agent dispatch status, synthesis summaries, and transition markers during all phases
README.md with installation guide, quick start, invocation modes, cycle phases, and project structure
Pre-flight check during onboarding warns if superpowers skills are missing
Adaptive research agent selection — dispatches 3-5 agents per cycle based on product stage and tech stack
Marketplace-ready plugin metadata with author, repository, homepage, keywords in plugin.json and self-hosted marketplace.json
Confidence-gated auto-approval mode — auto mode with configurable threshold auto-approves high-priority PRDs while routing lower-scored ones to collaboration
Post-execution satisfaction signal — 1-5 rating prompt after Update Memory phase for default/auto modes, stored in cycle logs and satisfaction_history
State validation before phase routing — validates phase enum, mode enum, and current_prd path safety in Phase Router Step 3b
/decide reset command — clean re-onboarding with optional backlog preservation
/decide rollback command — reverts most recent cycle commit with confirmation and backlog rejection
Operator validation script (validate.sh) — checks state.json, backlog.json, memory.json, cycle logs, and cross-references
/decide status and /decide backlog commands — read-only inspection of operator state and queued work

feature_history.feature: Onboarding with automated repo analysis and user interview
Bash launcher script for force mode
/decide slash command for invoking operator from Claude Code
Secure force mode launcher with shell injection fix, paused state handling, and max-cycles limit
Quick onboarding mode with confidence-based summary and fallback
README.md with installation and usage guide
Marketplace-ready plugin metadata and self-hosted marketplace manifest

assumptions: Users have Claude Code installed with superpowers plugin
Target projects have a git repository initialized
known_gaps: No cost awareness for subagent dispatches
Never been tested against a real target codebase
Launcher script still used for force mode continuous loop (acceptable, now secured)

past_decisions.decision: Renamed skill to 'decide' for /decide slash command. Skill now invocable natively in Claude Code.
Rewrote force mode launcher to fix critical shell injection and ensure full SKILL.md instruction loading. Added paused state handling and max-cycles safety limit.
Replaced 11-question sequential onboarding with confidence-scored summary table. Users confirm/correct in 2-3 exchanges. Fallback to full interview for empty repos.
past_decisions.lessons: Repo analysis output maps cleanly to all onboarding fields but revenue model and git tracking always need explicit asking.

past_decisions.decision: Added progress indicators to all phases — banners, dispatch status, synthesis summaries, transition markers.
Added README.md with installation, quick start, invocation modes, cycle phases, and project structure.
Added pre-flight check during onboarding that warns if superpowers skills are missing.
Research phase now selects 3-5 agents based on product stage and tech stack instead of all 7.
Added marketplace metadata to plugin.json, created marketplace.json manifest, and updated README to lead with /plugin install.
Added auto mode with configurable threshold for confidence-gated PRD approval. Third mode alongside default and force.
Added satisfaction rating prompt (1-5) to Update Memory phase for default/auto modes. Stored in cycle logs and satisfaction_history in memory.json. Force mode auto-skips.
Added state.json validation in Phase Router Step 3b. Checks phase enum, mode enum, and current_prd path safety. Self-heals with warnings.
Added /decide reset command with confirmation, optional backlog preservation, and force mode clean slate.
Added /decide rollback command that reverts most recent cycle's commit, confirms with user, marks backlog item as rejected.
Added validate.sh script that checks structural integrity of all .claude-operator/ state files. Also fixed schema drift in 2 old cycle logs.
past_decisions.lessons: Validation script immediately found real issues — early cycle logs used prd_filename instead of prd. Validates the value of structural validation.

past_decisions.decision: Added /decide status and /decide backlog read-only inspection commands.
